ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper provides a useful synthesis (14 CNN design patterns) and proposes several new architectural elements (Fractal of FractalNet, Stagewise Boosting Networks, Taylor Series Networks, freeze-drop-path), so it contains genuinely new ideas beyond pure survey. However, the novel architectures are evaluated only in limited, preliminary experiments on CIFAR-10/100 (one FoF realization) and several introduced variants (SBN, TSN) underperform the FractalNet baseline, suggesting incremental rather than transformative advances. Contributions are clearly differentiated from prior work, but the techniques appear to be modest extensions and would likely be of moderate interest to experts rather than surprising breakthroughs.

CLARITY.justification: The summary indicates a clear organization (survey of design patterns, introduction of novel modules, followed by experiments on CIFAR-10/100 and conclusions) and that the main contributions (14 design patterns, FoF/SBN/TSN, freeze-drop-path, and code release) are stated early. Notation consistency is not explicitly reported in the summary, but hyperparameters are documented and related work coverage is adequate, suggesting generally clear presentation. Some experimental choices are described as preliminary or underexplored (e.g., module dropout rates, limited FoF realization), implying minor presentation gaps rather than major clarity failures; overall writing quality is noted as good.

SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper's main claims are clearly stated and partially supported by experiments (e.g., FoF trains faster and attains similar final test accuracy to the FractalNet baseline on CIFAR-10/100), and there are no indicated mathematical derivations to check. However, the experimental methodology is limited/preliminary: evaluation is confined to one realization of FoF, uses only CIFAR datasets and a single baseline, reports no statistical testing, and admits some hyperparameters may be suboptimal. These limitations create some gaps in evidence and generalizability, so the conclusions are cautiously justified but not fully substantiated.

main_claims: Articulation of 14 CNN architectural design patterns distilled from recent literature for image classification.
Introduction of novel architectural elements: Fractal of FractalNet (FoF), Stagewise Boosting Networks (SBN), Taylor Series Networks (TSN), and the freeze-drop-path unit.
Empirical evaluation showing FoF trains faster and attains similar final test accuracy to the FractalNet baseline; SBN and TSN train faster but have lower final accuracy in the tested settings.
Provision of Caffe implementations and public release of code and prototxt files.
_summary.methodology: Survey recent CNN and Residual Network literature to extract 14 design patterns, then implement and experimentally validate selected architectural innovations (FoF, SBN, TSN, freeze-drop-path) by modifying a FractalNet baseline and evaluating on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100.
limitations: Experiments are preliminary and intended to validate ideas rather than provide a full evaluation.
Stagewise Boosting Network (SBN) and Taylor Series Network (TSN) lag behind FractalNet in final performance when using freeze-drop-path in the tested settings.
Freeze-drop-path usage is described as questionable for the evaluated tasks and left for future exploration.
Study is limited to convolutional neural networks for image classification and to one realization of the Fractal of FractalNet (FoF) design.
Some hyperparameter choices (e.g., module dropout rates) were not found by experimentation and may be suboptimal.
_summary.topic_area: CNN architecture design for image classification
